doctrine: AEG-HG-2B-006 — nonce protects the approval; idempotency protects the operation (separate locks)
soak_limitations: NonceStore under test is InMemoryNonceStore — single-process only; Redis (SET NX EX) required for multi-instance
Token format is dev base64url JSON; production tokens are JWT signed by AEGIS PROOF at port 4850
Concurrent serialisation check uses Promise.allSettled — in-process race; Redis is the true mutex in prod
Expired-nonce TTL test uses 1ms sleep window — timing-sensitive on loaded CI systems

results.label: 1. consumeNonce returns true on first use
2. consumeNonce returns false on second use (same nonce)
3. consumeNonce is nonce-scoped — different nonces are independent
4. expired nonce can be consumed again (TTL expired)
5. consumeNonce ttlMs=0 is accepted (token already at boundary)
6. triple call: first=true, second=false, third=false
7. store is per-instance — separate instances do not share state
8. consumeNonce is async (returns Promise)
9. high-frequency nonces do not collide with each other
10. happy path — token with nonce is accepted on first call
11. replay — same payload rejected on second call
12. missing nonce — token without nonce field rejected
13. replay error carries IRR-NOAPPROVAL code
14. IrrNoApprovalError on replay has code=IRR-NOAPPROVAL and doctrine=AEG-E-016
15. two different nonces in same store are independently accepted
16. two different nonces: second replay still rejected
17. nonce consumption happens before DB mutation — rejection is pre-commit
18. nonce in error message matches token nonce
19. verifyAndConsumeNonce does not emit SENSE on its own (observability separation)
20. nonce replay on expired token still rejected (cannot reuse expired approval)
21. nonce prevents approval replay (same token, second call rejects)
22. idempotency key (externalRef) is not the nonce — different fields, different purposes
23. a fresh nonce is required for each new approval, even for same operation
24. same externalRef with different nonces — idempotency layer is separate from nonce layer
25. nonce absent on token → rejected before DB regardless of externalRef presence
26. nonce is part of the approval scope — it binds to this specific approval instance
27. replay rejected even when externalRef is new (nonce gate is independent)
28. non-negotiable: promotion_permitted_carbonx = false
29. token issued_at far in future is rejected (forged timestamp)
30. token issued_at within CLOCK_SKEW_MS tolerance is accepted
31. token issued_at exactly at CLOCK_SKEW_MS boundary is accepted
32. future-token error message contains doctrine reference
33. non-negotiable: hard_gate_enabled = false (soft-canary enforced)
34. rejected replay throws before SENSE emission point (pre-mutation gate)
35. approved path (first use) does not throw — SENSE proceeds normally
36. nonce error carries IRR-NOAPPROVAL — distinguishable from generic errors
37. missing-nonce error is also IRR-NOAPPROVAL — same doctrine gate
38. non-negotiable: live HG-2B roster = 1 (parali-central only)
39. ttlMs = max(0, expires_at - now) — cannot be negative
40. ttlMs for valid token is positive (bounded by remaining lifetime)
41. nonce TTL ≤ token expiry — store cannot hold nonce past token lifetime
42. nonce TTL of 0 does not panic — max(0, ...) guard works
43. token with 5-min lifetime produces nonce TTL of ~300s (tolerance ±2s)
44. store throws → verifyAndConsumeNonce propagates the throw (fails CLOSED)
45. failing store does not return false (ambiguous) — it throws (unambiguous)
46. store throw is not swallowed — caller receives the error
47. operation gate: failing store blocks execution (no state mutation reaches DB)
48. InMemoryNonceStore never throws (in-process — always available)
49. token consumed on first obligation — second obligation with same token rejected
50. each obligation requires its own freshly-minted token (new nonce)
51. nonce serialises concurrent approvals — two goroutines cannot both win
52. digest of approval token is deterministic — same token always produces same ref
53. regression [Run 1]: hard_gate_enabled=false confirmed for this run
54. regression [Run 2]: wrong-scope token rejected before nonce check (scope is outer gate)
55. regression [Run 3]: digestApprovalToken produces 24-char hex (PRAMANA-safe ref)
56. regression [Run 3]: digest is not the raw token (secret hygiene)
57. overall: nonce and idempotency are separate locks — confirmed by type structure
